persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for date ideas — things two people would enjoy doing together.

Great date events:
- Food & drink: wine tastings, prix fixe dinners, cocktail classes, food festivals, tasting menus, new restaurant openings
- Arts & culture: gallery openings, museum exhibits, live music, jazz, comedy shows, theater, film screenings
- Experiences: cooking classes, pottery, dance lessons, sunset cruises, rooftop events, seasonal markets
- Outdoor: scenic walks, garden events, waterfront activities, bike tours
- Nightlife: speakeasy events, live DJ sets, themed parties, rooftop bars

Lower priority:
- Kids/family-focused events
- Pure networking or professional events
- Large-scale sports (unless it's a unique experience like courtside)
- Conferences, lectures, or panels with no social component

Score 0-10. Only return events with score >= 5.

events.name: Japan Festival! (Boston Commons)
events.description:
The Japan Festival Boston will once again be organized by the historic Boston Common on Saturday, April 25th, to Sunday, April 26th.
**This meetup is host-less, so use the comment section to find each other.** There will be performers, food vendors, cultural events, and more!

Japan’s traditional festival is a place to thank gods for their blessings and gather on special occasions with friends and family, serving as a catalyst for bonding communities. We would like to create our version of this
events.reason: Japan Festival offers food, culture, and outdoor activities — great date experience

events.name: Boards & Bonds: A Casual Evening of Board Games and D&D
events.description:
Come hang out with *Random Acts of Connection* for a chill evening of board games, D&D, and meeting cool new people at the CanalSide Food Hall in CambridgeSide. Whether you’re a tabletop pro or just curious to try something new, this is a fun, low-pressure way to connect, play, and maybe go on a wild quest or two. No experience needed—just bring yourself!

Dungeon Master Dan is guiding six souls on a one-shot Dungeon & Dragons campaign, Newbies welcome. Spots will be first-come based.

\*\*\*\*\
events.reason: Board games and D&D in a casual setting is a fun, interactive date experience

events.name: THE ANTLERS (18+)
events.description: THIS EVENT IS 18+ WITH A VALID ID. DOORS: 7:00 PM SHOW: 8:00 PM ALL TIMES AND SUPPORT BANDS ARE SUBJECT TO CHANGE WITHOUT NOTICE OR REFUND. Tickets purchased from unofficial third-party outlets CANNOT be verified by our Box Office. If you sell or purchase your tickets from a third-party, we are not liable for any entry issues you may have. Any disputes involving third-party ticket purchases must be settled with the third-party outlet directly. The Paradise Rock Club Box Office is open from 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM, Monday - Friday. We offer ticket purchases for Brighton Music Hall, Paradise Rock Club, Citizens House of Blues Boston, Orpheum Theatre, & MGM Music Hall at Fenway. If traveling from out of town, please call the customer line at 617-562-8801 to verify hours. There is a seating area available for those with mobility limitations. If you require accommodation, we encourage you to reach out to information@crossroadspresents.com as soon as you have purchased your GA ticket.

Be sure to visit this show's event details page immediately prior to each performance and to carefully read our pre-performance emails for important information pertaining to your performance. You may purchase tickets (free of service charges) in person at the Boch Center box office, located at 270 Tremont Street in Boston. The box office is open for advance sales Tuesday thru Saturday from 12-6pm and on the day of a show at least 2 hours prior and until at least one half-hour after the advertised show time. events.name: Heather McMahan: The Bamboozled Tour
events.description: Prices are subject to change at any time without notice. All sales are final. Please note the Shubert Theatre does not have an elevator; however, an accessible unisex bathroom is located off the Main Lobby. If you experience difficulty climbing stairs and wish to purchase seats in the Mezzanine or Balcony, please plan accordingly and arrive early to allow extra time to get to your seat. Restrictions and safety protocols vary by performance. Be sure to visit this show's event details page immediately prior to each performance and to carefully read our pre-performance emails for important information pertaining to your performance. Purchase tickets (free of service charges) in person at the Boch Center box office, open for advance sales Tue-Sat from 12-6pm and on the day of show at least 2 hours prior to at least one half-hour after the advertised show time. Some special offers, such as Platinum tickets, may not be available in person.

events.name: Pitch-a-Friend @ Aeronaut
events.description:
Let's go watch Pitch-a-Friend! I've gone once before and the slide presentations people put together to pitch their single friends can be fun, humorous, or heartwarming.

No need to be single to attend, it's just fun to watch. But for those who are single, there is an opportunity to mingle in the middle and after the presentations. The event is free and LGBTQ+ friendly.

***I recommend arriving as close to 7pm as possible since the event can reach capacity.

\~\~\~

Official event description:
events.reason: Pitch-a-Friend is entertaining, humorous, and social with mingling opportunities — unique date experience

events.name: Galantis ( 21+ )
events.description: This is a 21+ Event with valid form of identification. For US citizens, a valid DMV-issued photo ID or USA passport is required. For international guests, a valid passport is required. Doors 930pm All items brought onto the property are subject to inspection. Lighters, Cigarettes & Vapes are prohibited in the venue, there is NO SMOKING OR VAPING allowed at the venue per the city of Boston Ordinances. All bags larger than 10 in x 9 in, backpacks, professional cameras (i.e detachable lenses), video equipment, luggage, & like articles are strictly prohibited. Any medication must be accompanied with proof of prescription. Guests are prohibited from bringing weapons. Weapons include, but are not limited to, the following: firearms, explosives, stun guns, handcuffs, brass knuckles, sticks, clubs, batons, & knives. Patrons with prohibited articles will be turned away at the entrances and no storage or check area will be provided. Violation of the above will result in EJECTION.
